sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: KV Mechelen are in strong form, remaining unbeaten in their last 4 matches with a mix of wins and draws. Sint-Truidense VV, however, are struggling, having lost 3 of their last 5 matches and failing to win in their last 4 outings.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last 5 head-to-head matchups, Sint-Truidense VV have dominated with 4 wins and 1 draw. The most recent encounters suggest tight games, with the last match ending in a 1-1 draw.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: KV Mechelen have a solid defense, conceding only 8 goals (2nd best in the league), while Sint-Truidense VV have struggled defensively recently, conceding in all of their last 4 matches. Mechelen also create more big chances at home, which could be decisive.

sections.subtitle: Match Conditions
sections.text: Playing at home in Veoliastadion Achter De Kazerne gives KV Mechelen an edge, especially given their ability to score early and maintain defensive solidity. Sint-Truidense VV’s poor away form adds to the challenge for them.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, KV Mechelen leads Sint-Truidense VV by a narrow margin of 1-0. The home side managed to capitalize on a late first-half opportunity to take the lead. Despite Sint-Truidense VV showing more presence in the attacking third, Mechelen's defense held firm. Both teams have had their moments, but the game remains tightly contested with plenty of potential for shifts in momentum.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Sint-Truidense VV dominate the early stages with consistent attacking pressure. Their momentum was particularly strong in the opening minutes, maintaining a sustained push forward. However, Mechelen gradually found their footing, with notable attacking spells in the middle of the half. As the half progressed towards the closing stages, Mechelen gained significant momentum, culminating in their goal. Sint-Truidense VV attempted a late response before the whistle but couldn't convert their pressure into a goal.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key statistics breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Looking at the stats, Sint-Truidense VV showed a stronger attacking intent with more entries into the final third and penalty area compared to Mechelen. They also outperformed in terms of expected goals, indicating they created higher-quality chances but failed to finish them. Mechelen, on the other hand, relied on efficiency, converting one of their few clear opportunities. Possession was almost evenly split, with Mechelen slightly edging it. Defensively, Mechelen's backline has been under pressure but managed to limit Sint-Truidense VV's shots on target.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Player performances
halftimeSections.text: For Mechelen, Théence Koudou stood out with a key role in creating a big chance, showing creativity in attack. Lion Lauberbach also had a notable impact, though he missed a significant opportunity. On the Sint-Truidense VV side, Andrés Ferrari was a constant threat upfront, missing a big chance despite a high expected goals contribution. Rihito Yamamoto impressed with his ability to create openings through key passes. Both teams have players capable of turning the game in the second half if given the space.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Areas for improvement
halftimeSections.text: Mechelen needs to focus on creating more consistent attacking opportunities, as their first-half chances were limited despite taking the lead. Defensively, they must remain organized to counter Sint-Truidense VV's frequent final-third entries. For Sint-Truidense VV, finishing remains the critical area to address, as they squandered high-quality chances. They also need to capitalize on their aerial dominance to create more scoring threats from set pieces or crosses.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: The second half promises to be an intriguing battle. Sint-Truidense VV will likely push hard for an equalizer, leveraging their attacking intent from the first half. Mechelen, playing at home, will aim to protect their lead while looking for chances to extend it on the counter. Given Sint-Truidense VV's higher expected goals and missed opportunities, they seem poised to find the net if they sharpen their finishing. However, Mechelen's defensive resilience and home advantage could help them hold on or even add to their tally.
